summary: Sort gen-suggest rows by score BEFORE the --available-only RDAP filter loop, so the count early-exit returns the top-N highest-scoring available domains.
reason: gen.Generate emits candidates alphabetically; the availableOnly loop iterated in that order and broke at `count` matches, so users got the first N alphabetically-available domains rather than the top-N by brandability score (which --include-score=true makes the implicit goal).
validated_outcome: go test ./... passes; alphabetical-vs-score selection bias removed. The standalone `else if includeScore` sort block became unnecessary and was inlined into the unconditional pre-filter sort.
